author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
A Treatise on the Gift of Perseverance.
The Adversaries Cannot Deny Predestination to Those Gifts of Grace Which They Themselves Acknowledge, and Their Exhortations are Not Hindered by This Predestination Nevertheless.
originalBook: A Treatise on the Gift of Perseverance.
title: The Adversaries Cannot Deny Predestination to Those Gifts of Grace Which They Themselves Acknowledge, and Their Exhortations are Not Hindered by This Predestination Nevertheless.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: And what I said of chastity, can be said also of faith, of piety, of love, of perseverance, and, not to enumerate single virtues, it may be said with the utmost truthfulness of all the obedience with which God is obeyed.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: But those who place only the beginning of faith and perseverance to the end in such wise in our power as not to regard them as God’s gifts, nor to think that God works on our thoughts and wills so as that we may have and retain them, grant, nevertheless, that He gives other things,—since they are obtained from Him by the faith of the believer.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Why are they not afraid that exhortation to these other things, and the preaching of these other things, should be hindered by the definition of predestination?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Or, perchance, do they say that such things are not predestinated?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Then they are not given by God, or He has not known that He would give them.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Because, if they are both given, and He foreknew that He would give them, certainly He predestinated them.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: As, therefore, they themselves also exhort to chastity, charity, piety, and other things which they confess to be God’s gifts, and cannot deny that they are also foreknown by Him, and therefore predestinated; nor do they say that their exhortations are hindered by the preaching of God’s predestination, that is, by the preaching of God’s foreknowledge of those future gifts of His: so they may see that neither are their exhortations to faith or to perseverance hindered, even although those very things may be said, as is the truth, to be gifts of God, and that those things are foreknown, that is, predestinated to be given; but let them rather see that by this preaching of predestination only that most pernicious error is hindered and overthrown, whereby it is said that the grace of God is given according to our deservings, so that he who glories may glory not in the Lord, but in himself.
